text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation, often called RAG, combines information retrieval with text generation. A retriever finds relevant context from a document collection, and a language model uses that context to answer the user question.

RAG systems should be evaluated with retrieval metrics and generation metrics. Retrieval quality can be measured with hit rate, recall at k, precision at k, and mean reciprocal rank. Generation quality can be measured with faithfulness, groundedness, correctness, and answer relevancy.
Chunking is the process of splitting documents into smaller passages before embedding. Chunk size and chunk overlap can strongly affect retrieval performance, latency, and cost.
